---
title: "Smallfruit Primrose-Willow (Ludwigia microcarpa)"
slug: "ludwigia-microcarpa"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474882870/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Smallfruit Primrose-Willow"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Myrtales"
tax_family: "Onagraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Myrtales", "Onagraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474882870/medium.jpg" alt="Smallfruit Primrose-Willow" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/mj1387">
mj1387</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264452137">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Myrtales
-   **Family:** Onagraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=133289)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, pond margins, and wet ditches.

## Natural history notes

A wetland plant characterized by its slender stems, narrow leaves, and small yellow flowers followed by relatively small seed capsules. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, pond margins, and wet ditches. It grows during the warmer months and often occurs in shallow water or seasonally flooded areas, contributing to aquatic and wetland plant communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/133289-Ludwigia-microcarpa)
-   [Local profile](https://plant-directory.ifas.ufl.edu/plant-directory/ludwigia-peruviana/)
